fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Maine Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Maine — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license maine, dental assistant certification maine, RDA maine, DANB CDA maine, dental assistant radiography maine

board.name: Maine Board of Dental Practice
board.mailing_address: Maine Board of Dental Examiners, 35 State House Station, Augusta, ME 04333-0035

quick_facts.exam_name: DANB CDA exam (for Registered Dental Assistant)
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 12 hours per renewal cycle (for Registered Dental Assistant)

license_types.title: Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Maine.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet one of Maine's pathways to licensure: either completion of a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR 24 months (3500 hours) of full-time clinical dental assisting experience, AND successful completion of the DANB Radiation Health and Safety (RHS) and Infection Control (ICE) exams. Alternatively, holding a DANB Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) certification fulfills the examination requirement.

steps.title: Register for an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal and click 'Register a new account' to create your applicant profile, setting up your username and password.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal, select 'Dental Assistant' as your profession, and initiate a new application, carefully filling out all required sections.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documentation, such as official transcripts from your CODA-accredited program, DANB exam score reports, and employer verification forms, directly through the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal or arrange for official submission where required.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable $20.00 application fee electronically through the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: After submitting your complete application and all required documents, the Maine Board of Dental Examiners will review your materials. You may be contacted for additional information if needed.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Maine Board of Dental Practice by license.

faqs.question: Does Maine require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Maine requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Maine?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Maine.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Maine?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Maine?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $271. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Maine does not license general dental assistants. EFDA license required for expanded functions.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Maine
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in Maine, you must register and submit your application through the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal.

official_links.portal_name: Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services
official_links.registration_steps: First-time users must register for an account by clicking 'Register a new account' on the Maine Professional & Financial Regulation Online Services portal. This involves providing personal details, creating a username and password, and verifying the email address.
